event: The Wedding of Tobias and Sarah
symbols: wedding feast — the sacred covenant of marriage blessed by God
gathered company celebrating — the community witnessing and blessing the union
dog at center — the faithful dog that accompanied Tobias on his journey (Tobit 6:2)
canopy and drapery — the marriage bower, God's protective covering over the couple
description: A lively interior scene of a wedding celebration. Many figures are gathered in a dimly lit room with draped fabrics and a canopy. At center, a couple appears to be at the focus of the celebration. Around them, guests dance, play music, and feast. A dog is visible at center. An elderly figure writes or reads at a table to the right. The atmosphere is bustling and festive, with warm candlelit tones. Figures are dressed in 17th-century Dutch clothing. The palette is warm browns and golds with accents of white, red, and blue.
theological_description: The Book of Tobit narrates the marriage of young Tobias to Sarah, a woman whose seven previous husbands had been killed by the demon Asmodeus on their wedding nights (Tobit 3:8). Guided by the angel Raphael in disguise, Tobias prays on the wedding night and the demon is driven away (Tobit 8:2-3). Their marriage prayer is one of the most beautiful in Scripture: 'Blessed are you, O God of our ancestors... You made Adam, and for him you made his wife Eve as a helper and support. From the two of them the human race has sprung' (Tobit 8:5-6). This story teaches that marriage is under divine protection and that prayer overcomes the forces of evil. The wedding feast echoes the broader biblical theme of marriage as a sign of God's covenant love — from the Garden of Eden (Genesis 2:24) to the Marriage Supper of the Lamb (Revelation 19:9).

scripture_refs.reason: Tobias and Sarah's wedding night prayer that drives away the demon and consecrates their marriage
A man shall leave his father and mother and be united to his wife — the foundational marriage text
Blessed are those invited to the marriage supper of the Lamb — the eschatological wedding feast
